purpose: Provision and release the canonical MMG subscription-commerce stack through an approval-gated, idempotent, observable, rollback-aware deployment control plane without exposing Shopify, database, customer, storage, or staging-host credentials.

shopify_contract.publication_rule: Keep the product DRAFT until all end-to-end and controlled staging-rehearsal checks pass, then activate and publish only to the verified Online Store publication through the separate publish action.

rollback_contract.product_rollback: Return the product to DRAFT or unpublish it; do not delete customer contracts or delivered ownership.
rollback_contract.webhook_rollback: Release a prior app configuration version or disable the affected route after preserving the webhook inbox.
rollback_contract.database_rollback: Use forward repair migrations for applied commerce schemas unless a reviewed migration-specific rollback is approved.
rollback_contract.customer_rights: Never revoke delivered ownership solely because a deployment is rolled back.

privacy_and_security_contract: Never commit Shopify app secrets, Admin API tokens, database credentials, CSRF secrets, storage signing keys, operations credentials, staging-integration credentials, rehearsal credentials, provider-health credentials, Cloudflare credentials, or dispatcher secrets.
Authenticate deployment, operations, staging-readiness, staging-integration, provider-heartbeat, rehearsal, rehearsal-adapter, and runtime-control endpoints with their governed credentials and roles.
Require an additional production release-manager role for production actions.
Require production verification approval because controlled end-to-end verification may create a live test order and mutate entitlement state.
Do not accept live publication as part of general provisioning; publication is a separate approved action.
Require fresh release-bound and environment-bound end-to-end and staging-rehearsal evidence before publication.
Persist runtime GIDs and hashed test references, but never persist raw access tokens, checkout tokens, test customer references, raw test order identifiers, alert destinations, or provider payloads.
Use idempotent request IDs and optimistic release versions for every action.
Preserve immutable audit events for every phase transition and failure.
release_gates: The Shopify app has protected subscription API access and every required scope.
Migrations 001 through 011 are applied in order to the target PostgreSQL environment.
All required runtime routes, including readiness and provider-heartbeat refresh, are deployed and private routes enforce their specified controls.
The isolated Cloudflare staging host uses Hyperdrive and a staging-only service binding and reports the exact release with publication and live-customer authority disabled.
The staging integration composition root is connected to PostgreSQL, route probes, runtime controls, alerts, release evidence, rehearsal fixtures, and separate Admin Portal authentication.
The production operations composition root is connected to PostgreSQL telemetry, route probes, runtime controls, alerts, release evidence, and Admin Portal authentication.
The canonical product is provisioned as DRAFT with exact variants, prices, SKUs, nonshipping flags, metafields, and one shared monthly selling plan.
At least two digital assets pass subscription selection, media, package, secure-file, and recommendation metadata gates.
Customer Portal modules are inserted additively without replacing existing workflows.
The app-specific subscription webhooks are released on API version 2026-07.
The delivery scheduler, idempotent dispatcher, acknowledgement path, monitoring, alerting, and storage signer are active.
The complete SEV1, SEV2, consistency, rights-preservation, and staged-rollout rehearsal passes in staging for the exact release within 24 hours.
The full controlled end-to-end verification suite passes for the same release and environment within 24 hours of publication.
Publication receives a distinct executive approval bound to the exact release commit.

integration_sequence.previous_component: Staging readiness inspector and integration execution control
integration_sequence.current_component: Cloudflare staging host runtime with Hyperdrive, service binding, provider heartbeat refresh, and controlled deployment
integration_sequence.next_component: Deploy the staging upstream and host, configure protected infrastructure, then execute and review the first exact-release rehearsal without publishing Shopify resources
